description: 30-day elimination diet focusing on whole foods to reset eating habits and identify food sensitivities
restrictions: No sugar or sweeteners of any kind
No recreating baked goods or treats

w30_veggie_scramble.name: Loaded Veggie Scramble
w30_veggie_scramble.description: Fluffy scrambled eggs packed with bell peppers, mushrooms, and spinach cooked in ghee
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Melt ghee in a nonstick skillet over medium heat.
Sauté bell peppers and mushrooms for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Add spinach and cook until wilted, about 1 minute.
Whisk eggs with garlic powder, salt, and pepper, then pour into the skillet.
Gently stir eggs until just set, about 2-3 minutes. Serve immediately.

w30_sweet_potato_hash.name: Sweet Potato Breakfast Hash
w30_sweet_potato_hash.description: Crispy sweet potato cubes with Italian sausage, peppers, and a fried egg on top
ingredients: 1 medium sweet potato, diced into 1/2-inch cubes
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Heat avocado oil in a large cast-iron skillet over medium-high heat.
Add sweet potato cubes and cook for 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally, until golden and tender.
Push potatoes to one side, crumble sausage into the pan, and cook for 4-5 minutes until browned.
Add bell pepper and onion, season with smoked paprika, salt, and pepper, and cook 3 more minutes.
Make a well in the center and crack the egg into it. Cover and cook until egg white is set, about 3 minutes.

w30_banana_egg_pancakes.name: Banana Egg Pancakes with Berries
w30_banana_egg_pancakes.description: Simple two-ingredient pancakes made with banana and eggs, served with fresh mixed berries
ingredients: 1/2 cup fresh mixed berries (blueberries, raspberries, strawberries)
instructions: Mash bananas in a bowl until smooth, then whisk in eggs and cinnamon until combined.
Heat coconut oil in a nonstick skillet over medium-low heat.
Pour about 3 tablespoons of batter per pancake. Cook for 2-3 minutes until edges set and bubbles form.
Flip gently and cook another 1-2 minutes until golden.
Serve topped with fresh mixed berries.

w30_avocado_egg_cups.name: Baked Avocado Egg Cups
w30_avocado_egg_cups.description: Creamy avocado halves baked with eggs and topped with everything bagel seasoning
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F. Scoop out a little extra avocado flesh to make room for the eggs.
Place avocado halves in a muffin tin to keep them stable. Crack one egg into each half.
Season with salt, pepper, and everything bagel seasoning.
Bake for 14-18 minutes until egg whites are set but yolks are still slightly runny.
Top with fresh chives and hot sauce if desired.

w30_sausage_frittata.name: Sausage and Kale Frittata
w30_sausage_frittata.description: Hearty oven-baked frittata loaded with compliant sausage, kale, and roasted red peppers
ingredients: 1/3 cup diced roasted red peppers (packed in water)
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F. Heat ghee in an oven-safe skillet over medium heat.
Crumble sausage into the skillet and cook until browned, about 5 minutes. Add onion and cook 2 more minutes.
Add kale and roasted red peppers, stirring until kale wilts, about 2 minutes.
Whisk eggs with oregano, salt, and pepper, then pour evenly over the skillet contents.
Transfer skillet to the oven and bake for 15-18 minutes until eggs are set and top is lightly golden.
Let cool 5 minutes before slicing into wedges. Serves 2-3.

w30_coconut_chia_pudding.name: Coconut Chia Pudding
w30_coconut_chia_pudding.description: Creamy overnight chia pudding made with full-fat coconut milk and topped with toasted coconut flakes
ingredients: 3/4 cup full-fat coconut milk (canned, no additives)
instructions: In a jar or bowl, whisk together coconut milk, water, vanilla extract, and cinnamon.
Stir in chia seeds thoroughly to prevent clumping.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight.
Stir well before serving and top with toasted coconut flakes and fresh raspberries.

w30_smoked_salmon_plate.name: Smoked Salmon Breakfast Plate
w30_smoked_salmon_plate.description: Wild-caught smoked salmon with sliced avocado, cucumber, capers, and fresh dill over greens
ingredients: 4 oz wild-caught smoked salmon (sugar-free, no additives)
instructions: Arrange mixed greens on a plate as a base.
Layer smoked salmon slices, avocado, and cucumber on top.
Scatter capers and fresh dill over the plate.
Drizzle with olive oil and lemon juice, then finish with cracked black pepper.

w30_pork_apple_patties.name: Pork and Apple Breakfast Patties
w30_pork_apple_patties.description: Savory-sweet ground pork patties with shredded apple and sage, pan-fried until golden
ingredients: 1/2 medium Granny Smith apple, peeled and grated
instructions: In a bowl, combine ground pork, grated apple, sage, garlic powder, nutmeg, salt, and pepper. Mix gently until just combined.
Form mixture into 4 equal patties, about 1/2 inch thick.
Heat avocado oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Cook patties for 5-6 minutes per side until cooked through and golden brown.
Rest for 2 minutes before serving. Pairs well with sautéed greens or a fried egg.

w30_green_smoothie.name: Green Power Smoothie
w30_green_smoothie.description: Nutrient-dense smoothie with spinach, banana, coconut milk, and almond butter
ingredients: 1 tbsp almond butter (no sugar added, no peanuts)
instructions: Add coconut milk, water, and spinach to a blender and blend until smooth.
Add frozen banana, pineapple chunks, almond butter, and ginger.
Blend on high for 60-90 seconds until completely smooth and creamy.
Pour into a glass and serve immediately.

w30_turkey_breakfast_bowl.name: Turkey Breakfast Power Bowl
w30_turkey_breakfast_bowl.description: Seasoned ground turkey over roasted sweet potatoes with sautéed greens and a drizzle of tahini
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Toss diced sweet potato with half the avocado oil, salt, and pepper. Spread on a baking sheet and roast for 20-25 minutes until tender and caramelized.
While potatoes roast, heat remaining avocado oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Cook ground turkey, breaking it up, for 5-6 minutes until browned.
Season turkey with cumin, smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Add baby spinach to the skillet and stir until wilted, about 1 minute.
Assemble bowl with roasted sweet potatoes, seasoned turkey and spinach, and drizzle with tahini.

w30_prosciutto_egg_wrap.name: Prosciutto Egg Wrap
w30_prosciutto_egg_wrap.description: Thin egg crepes wrapped around prosciutto, arugula, and avocado for a grain-free breakfast wrap
instructions: Whisk eggs with a pinch of salt. Heat ghee in a large nonstick skillet over medium-low heat.
Pour eggs into the skillet and swirl to create a thin, even layer. Cook without stirring for 2-3 minutes until set.
Carefully flip and cook 30 seconds more. Slide onto a plate.
Spread Dijon mustard over the egg crepe. Layer prosciutto, avocado slices, and arugula on one side.
Roll up tightly and slice in half on a diagonal.

w30_plantain_hash_eggs.name: Plantain Hash with Eggs
w30_plantain_hash_eggs.description: Crispy fried green plantain cubes with seasoned ground beef, peppers, and two sunny-side eggs
ingredients: 1 large green plantain, peeled and diced
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Heat 1 tbsp coconut oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add diced plantain and cook for 8-10 minutes, turning occasionally, until golden and crispy.
Push plantain to the sides, add ground beef to the center, and cook 4-5 minutes until browned. Season with cumin, chili powder, salt, and pepper.
Add bell pepper and onion, toss everything together, and cook 2 more minutes.
Make two wells in the hash, add remaining coconut oil, and crack an egg into each well.
Cover skillet and cook for 3 minutes until egg whites are set. Serve from the skillet.

w30_chicken_caesar_wrap.name: Chicken Caesar Collard Wrap
w30_chicken_caesar_wrap.description: Grilled chicken with dairy-free caesar dressing wrapped in large collard green leaves
ingredients: 2 large collard green leaves, stems trimmed flat
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Season chicken breast with salt and pepper. Heat olive oil in a grill pan over medium-high heat and cook chicken 5-6 minutes per side until internal temp reaches 165°F. Let rest 5 minutes, then slice.
Make the dressing: whisk together mayo, lemon juice, Dijon, minced garlic, nutritional yeast, salt, and pepper.
Blanch collard leaves in boiling water for 30 seconds, then shock in ice water. Pat dry.
Lay collard leaves flat, spread dressing down the center, and top with sliced chicken and cherry tomatoes.
Roll up tightly, tucking in the sides. Slice in half to serve.

w30_tuna_stuffed_avocado.name: Tuna-Stuffed Avocados
w30_tuna_stuffed_avocado.description: Wild-caught tuna salad with celery and herbs nestled in creamy avocado halves
ingredients: 1 can (5 oz) wild-caught albacore tuna in water, drained
Salt and black pepper to taste
Pinch of smoked paprika for garnish
instructions: Drain tuna and flake into a medium bowl with a fork.
Add mayo, celery, red onion, lemon juice, dill, salt, and pepper. Mix gently until combined.
Scoop a little extra avocado flesh from each half to create a larger well.
Divide tuna mixture evenly among the four avocado halves.
Dust with smoked paprika and serve immediately.

w30_thai_coconut_soup.name: Thai Coconut Chicken Soup
w30_thai_coconut_soup.description: Aromatic coconut soup with shredded chicken, mushrooms, and lime in a fragrant lemongrass broth
ingredients: 1 stalk lemongrass, cut into 2-inch pieces and bruised
Fresh cilantro and lime wedges for serving
instructions: In a pot, bring coconut milk and bone broth to a simmer over medium heat. Add lemongrass and ginger slices.
Add chicken thighs whole to the pot and simmer for 15 minutes until cooked through.
Remove chicken, shred with two forks, and return to the pot.
Add mushrooms, coconut aminos, and red pepper flakes. Simmer 5 more minutes.
Remove lemongrass pieces and ginger slices. Stir in lime juice.
Serve in bowls topped with fresh cilantro and lime wedges.

w30_steak_salad.name: Grilled Steak Salad
w30_steak_salad.description: Seared flank steak over mixed greens with roasted sweet potato, red onion, and balsamic vinaigrette
ingredients: 1 small sweet potato, diced and roasted
1 tbsp balsamic vinegar (Whole30-compliant, no added sugar)
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Season flank steak generously with salt and pepper. Let come to room temperature for 10 minutes.
Roast diced sweet potato at 400°F with a drizzle of olive oil for 15-18 minutes until tender.
Sear steak in a hot cast-iron skillet for 4-5 minutes per side for medium-rare. Rest 5 minutes, then slice thinly against the grain.
Whisk together olive oil, balsamic vinegar, Dijon mustard, salt, and pepper to make the vinaigrette.
Arrange greens on a plate, top with sliced steak, roasted sweet potato, red onion, and radishes. Drizzle with vinaigrette.

w30_zucchini_turkey_boats.name: Turkey Taco Zucchini Boats
w30_zucchini_turkey_boats.description: Baked zucchini halves loaded with seasoned ground turkey, salsa, and guacamole
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Scoop out the center of each zucchini half to create boats. Place on a lined baking sheet.
Heat avocado oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Cook ground turkey for 5-6 minutes, breaking it apart, until browned.
Season turkey with chili powder, cumin, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Stir in salsa and diced tomatoes.
Fill each zucchini boat with the turkey mixture. Bake for 18-20 minutes until zucchini is tender.
Top with mashed avocado and fresh cilantro before serving.

w30_shrimp_cauliflower_rice.name: Shrimp Cauliflower Fried Rice
w30_shrimp_cauliflower_rice.description: Quick stir-fried cauliflower rice with plump shrimp, vegetables, and coconut aminos
ingredients: 8 oz large shrimp, peeled and deveined
1/2 cup frozen peas (Whole30-compliant: check no additives)
instructions: Heat avocado oil in a large wok or skillet over high heat. Sear shrimp for 2 minutes per side until pink. Remove and set aside.
Add garlic and ginger to the wok, stir for 30 seconds. Add carrots and cook 2 minutes.
Push veggies aside, scramble the beaten eggs on the empty side of the wok, then mix together.
Add riced cauliflower and peas. Stir-fry for 4-5 minutes until cauliflower is tender.
Return shrimp to the wok, add coconut aminos and sesame oil, toss everything together.
Serve topped with sliced green onions.

w30_chicken_avocado_blt.name: Chicken Avocado BLT Lettuce Wraps
w30_chicken_avocado_blt.description: Crispy bacon, grilled chicken, avocado, and tomato in butter lettuce cups with ranch drizzle
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Cook bacon in a skillet over medium heat until crispy, about 4-5 minutes per side. Drain on paper towels.
Season chicken breast with salt and pepper. Grill or pan-sear 5-6 minutes per side until cooked through. Slice.
Arrange butter lettuce leaves as cups on a plate.
Layer sliced chicken, crumbled bacon, avocado slices, and cherry tomatoes in each cup.
Drizzle with Whole30-compliant ranch dressing and serve.

w30_mediterranean_bowl.name: Mediterranean Salmon Bowl
w30_mediterranean_bowl.description: Herb-crusted baked salmon over cauliflower tabbouleh with olives, cucumbers, and tahini dressing
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Season salmon with oregano, salt, pepper, and a drizzle of olive oil. Bake for 14-16 minutes until flaky.
Sauté riced cauliflower in a dry pan over medium heat for 4-5 minutes until slightly dried out. Transfer to a bowl and cool.
Toss cooled cauliflower rice with cucumber, olives, red onion, parsley, mint, lemon juice, and remaining olive oil.
Whisk tahini with 1 tbsp water and a squeeze of lemon until smooth and drizzle-able.
Arrange cauliflower tabbouleh in a bowl, top with baked salmon, and drizzle with tahini dressing.

w30_egg_roll_bowl.name: Egg Roll in a Bowl
w30_egg_roll_bowl.description: Deconstructed egg roll with ground pork, shredded cabbage, and ginger-garlic coconut aminos sauce
instructions: Heat avocado oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Cook ground pork for 5-6 minutes, breaking apart, until browned. Drain excess fat.
Add garlic and ginger, stir for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add shredded cabbage and carrots. Stir-fry for 5-6 minutes until cabbage is wilted but still has some crunch.
Pour in coconut aminos and sesame oil, toss to coat evenly. Season with white pepper.
Serve in bowls topped with sliced green onions.

w30_chipotle_chicken_bowl.name: Chipotle Chicken Sweet Potato Bowl
w30_chipotle_chicken_bowl.description: Smoky chipotle-rubbed chicken over roasted sweet potatoes with cilantro-lime slaw
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F. Toss sweet potato cubes with 1/2 tbsp avocado oil and salt. Roast for 20-25 minutes until caramelized.
Mix chipotle powder, cumin, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and pepper. Rub over chicken thighs.
Heat remaining avocado oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Sear chicken 5 minutes per side until cooked through (165°F). Let rest, then slice.
Toss shredded cabbage with lime juice, cilantro, and a pinch of salt to make the slaw.
Assemble bowls with roasted sweet potato, sliced chipotle chicken, cilantro-lime slaw, and avocado slices.

w30_italian_meatball_soup.name: Italian Meatball and Vegetable Soup
w30_italian_meatball_soup.description: Tender Italian-seasoned meatballs simmered in a rich tomato broth with zucchini and spinach
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Mix ground beef, almond flour, egg, half the Italian seasoning, salt, and pepper. Form into 12-14 small meatballs.
Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Brown meatballs on all sides, about 4-5 minutes. Remove and set aside.
In the same pot, sauté onion and garlic for 2 minutes. Add diced tomatoes, bone broth, remaining Italian seasoning, and bring to a simmer.
Return meatballs to the pot, add zucchini, and simmer for 15 minutes until meatballs are cooked through.
Stir in fresh spinach until wilted. Season with salt and pepper.
Ladle into bowls and garnish with fresh basil.

w30_burger_lettuce_wrap.name: Classic Burger Lettuce Wraps
w30_burger_lettuce_wrap.description: Juicy grass-fed beef burgers in crisp lettuce wraps with all the fixings and special sauce
ingredients: 6 oz ground beef (grass-fed), formed into a patty
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Season the burger patty generously with salt and pepper on both sides.
Cook in a hot cast-iron skillet or grill over medium-high heat for 4-5 minutes per side for medium doneness.
Mix together mayo, mustard, and coconut aminos to create the special sauce.
Stack two lettuce leaves together for each wrap. Place cooked burger on lettuce, top with tomato, onion, pickles, and special sauce.
Wrap lettuce around the burger and secure with a toothpick if needed.

w30_lemon_herb_chicken.name: Lemon Herb Roasted Chicken Thighs
w30_lemon_herb_chicken.description: Juicy bone-in chicken thighs roasted with lemon, garlic, and fresh herbs over roasted vegetables
ingredients: 2 bone-in, skin-on chicken thighs (about 10 oz total)
1 medium lemon, half sliced, half juiced
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F. Toss broccoli and butternut squash with 1 tbsp olive oil, salt, and pepper on a sheet pan.
Season chicken thighs with salt, pepper, rosemary, and thyme. Nestle them skin-side up among the vegetables.
Scatter smashed garlic cloves and lemon slices around the pan. Drizzle chicken with remaining olive oil and lemon juice.
Roast for 35-40 minutes until chicken skin is golden and crispy, and internal temp reaches 175°F.
Let rest 5 minutes before serving directly from the sheet pan.

w30_salmon_asparagus.name: Pan-Seared Salmon with Roasted Asparagus
w30_salmon_asparagus.description: Crispy-skinned wild salmon with tender roasted asparagus and a bright lemon-caper pan sauce
ingredients: 6 oz wild-caught salmon fillet, skin on
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Toss asparagus with avocado oil, salt, and pepper on a baking sheet. Roast for 12-15 minutes until tender-crisp.
Pat salmon dry and season with salt and pepper. Heat 1 tbsp ghee in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Place salmon skin-side down and sear for 4 minutes. Flip and cook 3 more minutes until just cooked through. Remove from pan.
In the same skillet, add remaining ghee, garlic, and capers. Cook 30 seconds, then add lemon juice and swirl to combine.
Plate asparagus with salmon on top, spoon the lemon-caper sauce over everything, and serve with lemon wedges.

w30_beef_stir_fry.name: Beef and Broccoli Stir-Fry
w30_beef_stir_fry.description: Thinly sliced beef and crisp broccoli tossed in a savory coconut aminos and ginger sauce
ingredients: 8 oz flank steak, thinly sliced against the grain
1 tbsp arrowroot starch mixed with 2 tbsp water
instructions: Toss sliced beef with 1 tbsp coconut aminos and let sit while you prep other ingredients.
Heat avocado oil in a wok or large skillet over high heat until smoking. Sear beef in a single layer for 2 minutes, flip, and cook 1 more minute. Remove.
Add sesame oil to the wok, toss in broccoli and stir-fry 3-4 minutes until bright green and tender-crisp.
Add garlic, ginger, and red pepper flakes, stir 30 seconds. Return beef to the wok.
Pour in remaining coconut aminos and arrowroot slurry. Toss until sauce thickens and coats everything, about 1 minute.
Serve over cauliflower rice if desired. Garnish with sesame seeds.

w30_pork_carnitas.name: Slow-Cooker Pork Carnitas
w30_pork_carnitas.description: Fall-apart tender pork shoulder braised with citrus and spices, crisped under the broiler
ingredients: 2 lbs pork shoulder (bone-in or boneless), trimmed
Salt and black pepper to taste
Fresh cilantro and lime wedges for serving
instructions: Mix cumin, chili powder, oregano, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper. Rub generously all over the pork shoulder.
Place onion, garlic, and jalapeño in the bottom of a slow cooker. Set pork on top. Pour orange and lime juice over.
Cover and cook on LOW for 8 hours or HIGH for 4 hours until pork shreds easily.
Shred meat with two forks, discarding any large pieces of fat. Toss shredded pork in the cooking juices.
For crispy edges, spread shredded pork on a baking sheet and broil for 3-5 minutes until edges char.
Serve in lettuce cups or over cauliflower rice with cilantro and lime wedges. Serves 4-6.

w30_stuffed_peppers.name: Stuffed Bell Peppers
w30_stuffed_peppers.description: Colorful bell peppers filled with seasoned ground beef, cauliflower rice, and tomato sauce
ingredients: 3 large bell peppers (mixed colors), halved and seeded
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F. Place pepper halves cut-side up in a baking dish.
Heat avocado oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Sauté onion and garlic for 2 minutes, then add ground beef. Cook 5-6 minutes until browned.
Stir in cauliflower rice, half the marinara sauce, Italian seasoning, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper. Cook 2 minutes.
Fill each pepper half generously with the beef mixture. Spoon remaining marinara over the tops.
Cover with foil and bake for 25 minutes. Remove foil and bake 10 more minutes until peppers are tender.
Garnish with fresh basil. Serves 2-3.

w30_garlic_shrimp_zoodles.name: Garlic Shrimp with Zucchini Noodles
w30_garlic_shrimp_zoodles.description: Succulent garlic butter shrimp served over spiralized zucchini noodles with cherry tomatoes
ingredients: 8 oz large shrimp, peeled and deveined
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Pat shrimp dry and season with salt and pepper.
Melt 2 tbsp ghee in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add shrimp in a single layer and cook 2 minutes per side until pink. Remove.
Add remaining ghee and garlic to the skillet. Sauté 30 seconds until fragrant. Add cherry tomatoes and cook 1 minute.
Add zucchini noodles and toss for 2-3 minutes until just tender (don't overcook or they'll get watery).
Return shrimp to the pan, add lemon juice, red pepper flakes, and parsley. Toss to combine and serve immediately.

w30_tuscan_chicken.name: Tuscan Chicken with Sun-Dried Tomatoes
w30_tuscan_chicken.description: Creamy coconut milk-braised chicken with sun-dried tomatoes, spinach, and fresh basil
ingredients: 2 boneless skinless chicken breasts (about 12 oz total)
1/3 cup sun-dried tomatoes (oil-packed, check for Whole30 compliance)
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Season chicken breasts with Italian seasoning, salt, and pepper. Heat ghee in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Sear chicken for 5-6 minutes per side until golden and cooked through (165°F). Remove and set aside.
In the same skillet, add garlic and cook 30 seconds. Add sun-dried tomatoes and coconut milk. Stir in nutritional yeast.
Simmer for 3-4 minutes until sauce thickens slightly. Add spinach and stir until wilted.
Return chicken to the skillet, spooning sauce over the top. Simmer 2 more minutes.
Garnish with fresh basil and serve. Serves 2.

w30_lamb_chops.name: Herb-Crusted Lamb Chops with Mashed Cauliflower
w30_lamb_chops.description: Tender rack of lamb chops with a rosemary-garlic crust served over silky mashed cauliflower
ingredients: 1 large head cauliflower, cut into florets
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Boil cauliflower florets in salted water for 10-12 minutes until very tender. Drain well.
Mash cauliflower with 2 tbsp ghee, salt, and pepper using a potato masher or food processor until smooth and creamy.
Mix garlic, rosemary, thyme, olive oil, salt, and pepper into a paste. Rub all over lamb chops.
Heat remaining ghee in a cast-iron skillet over high heat. Sear lamb chops 3-4 minutes per side for medium-rare (145°F internal).
Let lamb rest for 5 minutes. Serve over a bed of mashed cauliflower.

w30_chicken_tikka.name: Chicken Tikka with Roasted Vegetables
w30_chicken_tikka.description: Warmly spiced chicken thighs with tikka masala seasoning served with roasted cauliflower and sweet potato
ingredients: 8 oz boneless skinless chicken thighs, cut into chunks
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Mix coconut milk, turmeric, garam masala, cumin, coriander, paprika, cayenne, garlic, ginger, and salt in a bowl. Toss chicken chunks in the marinade and let sit 10 minutes.
Preheat oven to 425°F. Toss sweet potato and cauliflower with 1 tbsp avocado oil and salt on a sheet pan. Roast for 20-25 minutes.
Heat remaining avocado oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Cook marinated chicken for 6-8 minutes, turning occasionally, until golden and cooked through.
Add any remaining marinade to the skillet and simmer 2 minutes until thickened.
Serve chicken over roasted vegetables, garnished with fresh cilantro.

w30_fish_tacos.name: Blackened Fish Tacos in Jicama Shells
w30_fish_tacos.description: Cajun-spiced white fish in crisp jicama taco shells with mango salsa and avocado crema
ingredients: 1 medium jicama, peeled and sliced into thin rounds
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Mix chili powder, cumin, smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Coat fish on all sides with the spice blend.
Make mango salsa: combine diced mango, red onion, cilantro, half the lime juice, and a pinch of salt.
Make avocado crema: blend avocado, coconut milk, remaining lime juice, and salt until smooth.
Heat avocado oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Cook fish 4-5 minutes per side until blackened and flaky.
Flake fish into large chunks. Serve in jicama rounds topped with mango salsa and avocado crema.

w30_pot_roast.name: Classic Pot Roast with Root Vegetables
w30_pot_roast.description: Melt-in-your-mouth beef chuck roast slow-braised with carrots, parsnips, and fresh herbs
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Season chuck roast generously with salt and pepper. Heat avocado oil in a large Dutch oven over high heat and sear the roast 3-4 minutes per side until deeply browned. Remove.
Add onion and garlic to the pot and cook 2 minutes. Stir in tomato paste and cook 1 minute more.
Pour in bone broth and scrape up any browned bits from the bottom.
Return the roast to the pot, add rosemary and thyme sprigs. Surround with carrots and parsnips.
Cover with a tight lid and braise in a 300°F oven for 3.5-4 hours (or slow cooker on LOW for 8 hours) until fork-tender.
Slice or shred meat, serve with vegetables and cooking liquid spooned over. Serves 4-6.

w30_turkey_meatloaf.name: Turkey Meatloaf with Roasted Brussels Sprouts
w30_turkey_meatloaf.description: Moist ground turkey meatloaf with a tangy tomato glaze alongside crispy halved Brussels sprouts
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F. Mix ground turkey, almond flour, egg, celery, onion, garlic, half the tomato paste, coconut aminos, thyme, salt, and pepper.
Shape mixture into a loaf on a parchment-lined baking sheet. Spread remaining tomato paste over the top.
Toss halved Brussels sprouts with avocado oil, salt, and pepper. Scatter around the meatloaf.
Bake for 40-45 minutes until meatloaf reaches 165°F internal and Brussels sprouts are golden and crispy.
Let meatloaf rest 10 minutes before slicing. Serves 3-4.

w30_seared_scallops.name: Seared Scallops with Butternut Squash Purée
w30_seared_scallops.description: Golden-crusted sea scallops served over velvety butternut squash purée with crispy prosciutto
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Boil butternut squash in salted water for 12-15 minutes until very tender. Drain and purée with 1 tbsp ghee, bone broth, nutmeg, salt, and pepper until silky smooth.
Crisp prosciutto slices in a dry skillet over medium heat for 2-3 minutes per side. Crumble and set aside.
Pat scallops very dry with paper towels and season with salt and pepper.
Heat remaining ghee in a skillet over high heat until smoking. Sear scallops 2 minutes per side without moving them — they should develop a deep golden crust.
Spoon butternut purée onto plates, arrange scallops on top, and finish with crumbled prosciutto and fried sage leaves.

w30_chicken_stir_fry.name: Cashew Chicken Stir-Fry
w30_chicken_stir_fry.description: Quick weeknight stir-fry with tender chicken, roasted cashews, and colorful vegetables in a savory sauce
ingredients: 8 oz boneless skinless chicken breast, cut into bite-size pieces
1 tbsp arrowroot starch mixed with 2 tbsp water
instructions: Toast cashews in a dry skillet over medium heat for 3-4 minutes, tossing frequently, until golden. Set aside.
Heat avocado oil in a wok over high heat. Season chicken with salt and stir-fry for 4-5 minutes until golden and cooked through. Remove.
Add snap peas, bell pepper, and water chestnuts to the wok. Stir-fry 2-3 minutes until crisp-tender.
Add garlic and ginger, cook 30 seconds. Return chicken to the wok.
Pour in coconut aminos, sesame oil, and arrowroot slurry. Toss until sauce thickens and coats everything.
Top with toasted cashews and green onions. Serve over cauliflower rice.

w30_balsamic_pork_chops.name: Balsamic Glazed Pork Chops with Roasted Beets
w30_balsamic_pork_chops.description: Thick-cut pork chops with a tangy balsamic reduction alongside sweet roasted beets and arugula
ingredients: 3 tbsp balsamic vinegar (Whole30-compliant, no sugar added)
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Toss cubed beets with 1 tbsp olive oil, salt, and pepper. Roast for 25-30 minutes until tender and caramelized.
Season pork chops generously with salt and pepper. Heat ghee in an oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat.
Sear pork chops 3 minutes per side until golden. Transfer skillet to the oven and cook 8-10 minutes until internal temp reaches 145°F.
Remove pork chops to rest. Place skillet back on stove over medium heat. Add garlic, cook 30 seconds, then pour in balsamic vinegar and thyme. Simmer 2-3 minutes until reduced and syrupy.
Toss arugula with remaining olive oil and a pinch of salt. Plate with roasted beets, pork chops, and drizzle the balsamic glaze over everything.

w30_guacamole.name: Classic Guacamole with Veggie Dippers
w30_guacamole.description: Chunky, zesty guacamole served with an assortment of fresh vegetable sticks for dipping
instructions: In a bowl, mash one avocado roughly and dice the other for a chunky texture.
Fold in red onion, jalapeño, cilantro, lime juice, cumin, and salt.
Taste and adjust seasoning — add more lime or salt as needed.
Serve immediately with carrot sticks, celery sticks, and cucumber slices.

w30_turkey_roll_ups.name: Turkey and Avocado Roll-Ups
w30_turkey_roll_ups.description: Whole30-compliant deli turkey rolled around avocado, cucumber, and mustard for a quick protein snack
ingredients: 4 oz Whole30-compliant deli turkey slices (no sugar, nitrate-free)
instructions: Lay turkey slices flat on a cutting board.
Spread a thin layer of Dijon mustard across each slice.
Place avocado slices and cucumber strips at one end of each turkey slice.
Roll up tightly and secure with a toothpick if needed. Serve immediately.

w30_deviled_eggs.name: Everything Bagel Deviled Eggs
w30_deviled_eggs.description: Classic deviled eggs made with compliant mayo and topped with everything bagel seasoning
instructions: Place eggs in a pot, cover with cold water. Bring to a boil, then cover, remove from heat, and let sit 12 minutes.
Transfer eggs to an ice bath for 5 minutes. Peel carefully.
Slice eggs in half lengthwise and scoop yolks into a bowl.
Mash yolks with mayo, Dijon, apple cider vinegar, and salt until smooth.
Pipe or spoon filling back into egg white halves. Sprinkle with everything bagel seasoning and paprika. Makes 12 halves.

w30_almond_butter_apple.name: Apple Slices with Almond Butter
w30_almond_butter_apple.description: Crisp apple slices paired with creamy almond butter and a sprinkle of cinnamon
ingredients: 1 large Honeycrisp apple, cored and sliced
instructions: Core and slice the apple into thin wedges.
Arrange apple slices on a plate with almond butter in a small dish for dipping.
Sprinkle cinnamon over the almond butter.

w30_coconut_energy_bites.name: Coconut Date Energy Bites
w30_coconut_energy_bites.description: No-bake energy bites made with dates, coconut, cashews, and a hint of vanilla
ingredients: 2 tbsp unsweetened coconut flakes for rolling
instructions: Place dates, cashews, shredded coconut, coconut oil, vanilla, and salt in a food processor.
Pulse until the mixture is finely chopped and sticks together when pressed between your fingers.
Roll into 10-12 small balls, about 1 tablespoon each.
Roll each ball in coconut flakes to coat.
Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to firm up. Store in the fridge for up to one week. Makes ~12 bites (serving: 3 bites).

w30_prosciutto_wrapped_melon.name: Prosciutto-Wrapped Melon Bites
w30_prosciutto_wrapped_melon.description: Sweet cantaloupe wrapped in salty prosciutto with a squeeze of fresh lime
instructions: Cut cantaloupe into 1-inch cubes.
Wrap each cube with a half-slice of prosciutto.
Arrange on a plate, squeeze fresh lime over the top.
Garnish with fresh mint leaves and cracked black pepper.

w30_sweet_potato_fries.name: Baked Sweet Potato Fries with Garlic Aioli
w30_sweet_potato_fries.description: Crispy oven-baked sweet potato fries with a creamy homemade garlic aioli dipping sauce
ingredients: 1 large sweet potato, cut into 1/4-inch fries
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F. Soak cut fries in cold water for 10 minutes, then pat completely dry.
Toss fries with avocado oil, smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Spread in a single layer on a parchment-lined baking sheet.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, flipping halfway, until golden and crispy on the edges.
Mix mayo, grated garlic, lemon juice, and a pinch of salt to make the aioli.
Serve fries hot with garlic aioli on the side.

w30_smoked_salmon_bites.name: Smoked Salmon Cucumber Bites
w30_smoked_salmon_bites.description: Refreshing cucumber rounds topped with smoked salmon, avocado mousse, and fresh dill
ingredients: 1 large English cucumber, cut into 1/2-inch thick rounds
instructions: Mash avocado with lemon juice, garlic powder, and a pinch of salt until smooth.
Arrange cucumber rounds on a serving plate.
Top each round with a small spoonful of avocado mousse.
Place a piece of smoked salmon on each cucumber round.
Garnish with fresh dill, capers, and cracked black pepper.

w30_plantain_chips_salsa.name: Baked Plantain Chips with Fresh Salsa
w30_plantain_chips_salsa.description: Thin crispy baked green plantain chips served with a bright pico de gallo
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F. Slice plantains paper-thin using a mandoline or sharp knife.
Toss plantain slices with avocado oil and sea salt. Arrange in a single layer on parchment-lined baking sheets.
Bake for 15-18 minutes, flipping halfway, until golden and crispy. Watch closely to prevent burning.
While chips bake, combine tomatoes, onion, cilantro, lime juice, jalapeño, and salt for the salsa.
Let chips cool for 5 minutes to crisp up further. Serve with fresh salsa.

w30_mixed_nuts_olives.name: Rosemary Roasted Nuts and Olives
w30_mixed_nuts_olives.description: Warm roasted mixed nuts and marinated olives with rosemary, garlic, and red pepper flakes
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. Spread nuts on a baking sheet and roast for 8-10 minutes until fragrant and lightly golden.
In a small skillet, warm olive oil over low heat with garlic, rosemary, and red pepper flakes for 2-3 minutes.
Toss warm roasted nuts and olives with the infused oil mixture.
Sprinkle with flaky sea salt and serve warm. Makes 2 servings.

w30_waldorf_chicken_salad.name: Whole30 Waldorf Chicken Salad
w30_waldorf_chicken_salad.description: Classic Waldorf salad reinvented with shredded chicken, crisp apples, grapes, celery, and walnuts in a creamy mayo dressing
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: In a large bowl, combine shredded chicken, diced apple, grapes, celery, and walnuts.
In a small bowl, whisk together mayo, lemon juice, celery seed, salt, and pepper.
Pour dressing over the chicken mixture and toss gently to coat.
Serve over a bed of mixed greens.
